title: Protect America’s Innovation and Economic Security from CCP Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 607.
summary: The "Protect America's Innovation and Economic Security from CCP Act" is a policy aimed at safeguarding US innovation and economic security from the potential threats of the Chinese Communist Party. It likely involves measures to counter China's influence, protect US intellectual property, and promote American competitiveness in key industries.

title: A resolution expressing support for the designation of the month of June 2026 as "National Post-Traumatic Stress Awareness Month" and June 27, 2026, as "National Post-Traumatic Stress Awareness Day".
action: Submitted in the Senate, considered, and agreed to without amendment and with a preamble by Unanimous Consent. (consideration: CR S2670; text: CR S2666)
summary: This policy calls for national recognition of post-traumatic stress awareness. It advocates for June 2026 to be "National Post-Traumatic Stress Awareness Month" and June 27, 2026, to be "National Post-Traumatic Stress Awareness Day."

title: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2026
action: Held at the desk.
summary: This policy document appears to be a law outlining the authorization and funding for the US military's defense programs and activities for the 2026 fiscal year. It likely includes provisions for military spending, personnel, procurement, and other defense-related matters.

title: A bill to expand the mission of the Export-Import Bank of the United States and focus on building export-related domestic critical industries that produce goods and services that support employment in the United States and strengthen global competitiveness, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs. (text: CR S2786-2791; Sponsor introductory remarks on measure: CR S2786)
summary: This bill aims to expand the Export-Import Bank's mission to support domestic critical industries that create US jobs and increase global competitiveness. The ultimate goal is to strengthen the US economy through exports and job creation.

title: Returning Senate Joint Resolution 3 to the Senate.
action: Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
summary: The document is about formally returning a proposed Senate resolution to its original place in the Senate process, allowing further consideration and action. It's a procedural step that doesn't necessarily imply approval or rejection of the resolution's contents.

title: Providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Internal Revenue Service relating to "Gross Proceeds Reporting by Brokers That Regularly Provide Services Effectuating Digital Asset Sales".
action: Became Public Law No: 119-5.
summary: This bill aims to block a new rule from the IRS regarding how digital asset sales are reported to the government. The proposed rule changes how brokers report gross proceeds from digital asset sales to the IRS.

title: Proposing an amendment to the Constitution of the United States to prohibit any person who has citizenship or nationality of, or otherwise owes allegiance to, a country other than the United States from serving as a Representative or Senator in Congress, a Judge of the Supreme Court or any inferior court, an Ambassador, public Minister or Consul, or any other officer of the United States which requires the advice and consent of the Senate, or the President or Vice President unless the person formally and permanently relinquishes such citizenship, nationality, or allegiance.
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: This amendment proposes adding a citizenship requirement to certain US government positions to ensure those serving have an exclusive allegiance to the United States. It aims to prevent people with dual citizenship or foreign allegiances from holding key government roles.

title: A joint resolution providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Bureau of Land Management relating to "National Petroleum Reserve in Alaska Integrated Activity Plan Record of Decision".
action: Became Public Law No: 119-47.
summary: This document outlines a US Congress attempt to reject a rule made by the Bureau of Land Management that involves a plan for the National Petroleum Reserve in Alaska. The plan aims to manage the reserve's activities.

title: Subpoena Abuse Prevention Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: The Subpoena Abuse Prevention Act aims to prevent misuse of subpoenas and protect individuals from undue investigation or prosecution. This likely involves setting limits or regulations to ensure law enforcement and other entities do not issue excessive or unjustified subpoenas.

title: Equitable Access to School Facilities Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 586.
summary: The Equitable Access to School Facilities Act is a policy aiming to ensure that all individuals have equal access to school facilities. However, due to the lack of description, it's unclear what specific facilities or circumstances the act addresses.

title: Critical Minerals Supply Chain Resiliency Act of 2026
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 602.
summary: The Critical Minerals Supply Chain Resiliency Act of 2026 aims to strengthen the security and reliability of critical minerals supply chains. This act likely focuses on measures to mitigate potential disruptions in the supply of essential minerals.

title: Empower Charter School Educators to Lead Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 379.
summary: The Empower Charter School Educators to Lead Act aims to empower educators at charter schools, but its specific details are not provided in the title or description.

title: Departments of Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2027
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 604.
summary: The Departments of Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2027 aims to provide funding for various government departments that support workforce development, healthcare, and education programs. It is an appropriations act that outlines how funds will be allocated to these departments for the fiscal year 2027.

title: A resolution expressing support for the designation of July 2026 as "National Sarcoma Awareness Month".
action: Submitted in the Senate, considered, and agreed to without amendment and with a preamble by Unanimous Consent. (consideration: CR S2705; text: CR S2703)
summary: This policy aims to express support for designating July 2026 as "National Sarcoma Awareness Month". The goal is to raise awareness about sarcomas, a rare type of cancer, during the designated month.

title: Military Readiness Permitting Efficiency Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Military Readiness Permitting Efficiency Act of 2026 aims to improve the efficiency of permitting processes for military readiness activities. This act likely seeks to facilitate smoother operations and quicker decision-making for military-related projects.

title: Prior Authorization Reform for Autoimmune and Blood Disorders Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committees on Education and Workforce, and Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Prior Authorization Reform for Autoimmune and Blood Disorders Act aims to simplify healthcare access for people with autoimmune and blood disorders by reducing bureaucratic hurdles in the approval process. The proposed legislation seeks to bring about more streamlined and efficient prior authorization requirements.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026 aims to address the environmental effects of artificial intelligence systems. This act likely establishes regulations to mitigate the negative environmental consequences of AI.

title: Right to Record Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: The Right to Record Act of 2026 is a law that protects the rights of individuals to record and capture images or sounds, likely in public places. This law likely imposes restrictions on who can restrict or prevent such recordings from being made, and may have implications for law enforcement, surveillance, and individual freedoms.

title: Powering the Future of American Space Dominance Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Powering the Future of American Space Dominance Act is a proposed law aimed at advancing American space capabilities. The exact details and goals of the act are unknown without further information.
